title: Back these dance buddies all the way to Spain
description: GEORGE NEWS - Two young dancers of the...
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE NEWS - Two young dancers of the Rhythmical Seeds dance company in George have set their sights on the Dance World Cup Finals in Spain in July.


> Judah Mkhambi (14) and Chloé Swanepoel (10) recently qualified with three and four solo items respectively, to represent South Africa at this prestigious event in Burgos.


“From choreography to discipline, heart to hard work, they’ve given it their all. Now it’s time to rally behind them!” says their coach, Lizette Gerber, Rhythmical Seeds’ owner.

Photo gallery: Rhythmical Seeds dancers excell at qualifiers for Dance World Cup Finals in Spain

Unfortunately, discipline, talent and hard work do not always equal bags of money and the community is called upon to support the two dancers' Back-a-Buddy campaigns to help them take their place on the world stage.


> Find their campaigns here:


- Judah Mkhambi
- Chloé Swanepoel

Read more in the paper that appears on Wednesday this week due to the public holiday on Good Friday.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
